followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I lower confidence and change the verdict from weak_evidence to contested because the other reviewers correctly identified that the current bundle contains no scientific body text. I do not adopt a score of 0 or treat the paper itself as technically void, because my prior cycle contains specific article-level notes that may have come from a fuller extraction; instead, the review record is internally inconsistent and needs human reconciliation.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Scoring the paper's scientific technical soundness, novelty, and significance as exactly 0 solely because the scrape failed conflates a review-process failure with an evaluation of the paper itself.

Treating EHT or GRAVITY concerns as confirmed major objections is not sufficiently location-grounded in the current bundle; they should be human spot-check questions unless the full paper omits or mishandles them.

Claims that the methods section is unavailable are true for the current bundle but conflict with my prior review's detailed notes; the right conclusion is an inconsistent review record, not automatic rejection of all prior line-grounded objections.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: The current supplied bundle is fatally incomplete for review: it contains only navigation/share UI text and no scientific content.

The broader RAR fermionic-dark-matter alternative to Sgr A* is an existing, contested research program, so novelty should be viewed as incremental unless the full paper shows a major new constraint or dataset.

It is reasonable to ask whether the full paper addresses EHT Sgr A* imaging and GRAVITY S2 precession constraints, because those are important domain constraints for any non-black-hole central-object model.

main_claims.claim: RAR fermionic core-halo configurations can fit the S2 orbit comparably to a black-hole potential while incorporating halo-scale mass constraints.
main_claims.notes: This was partially supported in my prior review from a fuller-looking extraction, but the current bundle cannot substantiate it. Needs verification against the actual paper text.
main_claims.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Abstract; Sections 2.1-3.1; especially lines 499-503, 533-540, 843-849. Current bundle: scientific body absent.

main_claims.claim: For the selected S2 data set, the 56 keV fermion model is favored over the 300 keV model with log Bayes factor B_1,2 = 10.1.
main_claims.notes: Even if the numerical value is present, the prior cycle concern remains: marginal likelihoods are not auditable unless prior supports, parameterization, and evidence-estimator uncertainty are stated.
main_claims.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Section 3.1 and Table 3; lines 850-851 and 701-713. Current bundle: scientific body absent.

main_claims.claim: G-object data do not yield a conclusive preference among black-hole and fermionic models.
main_claims.notes: The claim is plausible and cautious, but the plug-in treatment of central potentials should be checked in the full paper.
main_claims.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Section 3.2 and Discussion; lines 853-856 and 945-947. Current bundle: scientific body absent.

main_claims.claim: Changing outer-halo constraints to GAIA-DR3-like mass constraints leaves the fitted S2 orbital parameters essentially unchanged.
main_claims.notes: Cannot be verified from the current bundle. The prior review found the robustness claim insufficiently numerically documented.
main_claims.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Section 3.3; lines 918-923. Current bundle: scientific body absent.

main_claims.claim: For all stellar objects tested, black-hole and fermionic models predict orbital parameters differing by less than 1 per cent.
main_claims.notes: If my prior Table 5 transcription is correct, this headline claim is contradicted by listed G-object semimajor axes and periods differing by more than 1 per cent. This needs human spot-checking against the actual table.
main_claims.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Abstract line 503; Table 5 lines 864-873 and 878-885. Current bundle: scientific body absent.

missing_assumptions.assumption: A complete, accessible paper text or PDF is available for audit, and the current 305-character bundle is a scrape failure rather than the intended article content.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Current bundle metadata and Source Text.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Without the paper body, none of the line-level technical claims can be independently verified in this cycle.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Explicit bounds and parameterization for all uniform priors are provided somewhere in the full article, supplementary material, figures, or code.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Section 2.3 lines 637-642 and 837.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Bayesian evidences and Bayes factors are prior-volume sensitive and cannot be interpreted quantitatively without these details.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Observational errors in RA, Dec., and radial velocity can be treated as independent Gaussian errors with negligible covariance/systematic floors.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: Correlated astrometry or instrument systematics could materially change posterior widths and model evidences.

missing_assumptions.assumption: For G objects, uncertainty in the central potential inferred from S2 is negligible compared with G-object orbital-parameter uncertainty.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Section 2.3 lines 696-699; Section 3.2 line 853.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If central-potential uncertainty is not propagated, model comparison using G objects may be overconfident.

missing_assumptions.assumption: G objects can be modeled as geodesic test particles, with nongravitational forces, gas dynamics, and extended-source effects negligible for the data used.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Introduction lines 518-524; orbital dynamics Section 2.2 lines 619-633.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The physical nature of G objects is debated; purely gravitational test-particle fits may be incomplete.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Was my cycle-1 review based on a fuller extraction/PDF, or were the detailed line references inconsistent with the actual bundle?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The current prompt provides only the 305-character navigation scrape plus my prior review. I cannot reconstruct the missing scientific text from the supplied bundle.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the actual Table 5 contradict the abstract's '<1 per cent' orbital-parameter claim for G-object semimajor axes or periods?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires checking the real PDF/HTML table against the abstract wording; the current bundle lacks the table.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are prior ranges, sampler settings, convergence diagnostics, and MCEvidence uncertainties specified in the PDF, supplementary material, figure captions, or code?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The current bundle lacks methods content, and the prior cycle notes may not capture supplementary material.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the paper directly address EHT Sgr A* 2022 imaging constraints and the GRAVITY S2 Schwarzschild-precession measurement?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: These are domain-critical external constraints raised by another reviewer, but the current bundle does not permit a paper-location-grounded check.

novelty_assessment: Subject to the bundle defect, the work appears incrementally novel rather than field-shaping: it extends an existing RAR fermionic-dark-matter program to S2/G-source orbital fits, a higher-compactness case, and halo-constraint robustness checks. The other reviewers' observation that the broader RAR alternative-to-Sgr-A* program predates this paper is valid. Claims about EHT or GRAVITY constraints may be important context, but in this cycle they are not sufficiently paper-location-grounded to count as confirmed defects.
score_rationale: The score is lower than my cycle-1 score because the review record is now contaminated by a fatal bundle/provenance defect. I still do not assign a near-zero paper score: prior detailed notes suggest a real, concrete modeling paper with plausible but under-documented analyses. The main unresolved risks are reproducibility of Bayes factors, possible contradiction of the '<1 per cent' headline claim, plug-in treatment of G-object potentials, and the inability to verify any of this from the current source text.
summary: This follow-up cycle exposes a serious provenance problem: the supplied bundle text contains only OUP navigation chrome, while my cycle-1 review relied on detailed article content, tables, equations, and line references not present in the current bundle. I therefore cannot certify the whole paper or fully re-audit my prior line-grounded objections from the current evidence alone. Treating my prior notes as provisional notes from a fuller extraction, the paper appears to be an astrophysical/statistical modeling study comparing Schwarzschild black-hole and fermionic RAR dark-matter potentials for S2 and G objects near Sgr A*. The qualitative conclusion that current S-star/G-object orbits alone may not decisively distinguish the potentials remains plausible, but the quantitative Bayesian claims and one headline '<1 per cent' claim remain under-supported pending human verification against the full PDF/HTML.

technical_objections.evidence: The current Source Text contains only navigation/share UI strings such as 'Skip to Main Content', 'Article Navigation', 'PDF', and social sharing labels, with no abstract, methods, equations, figures, tables, or references.
technical_objections.objection: The review bundle currently lacks the scientific paper body, making independent follow-up audit impossible from the supplied source text.
technical_objections.paper_location: Current bundle Source Text and metadata: source_text_chars = 305; bundle_text_chars = 305.

technical_objections.evidence: My prior review noted Table 5 entries such as G2 a = 0.464 versus 0.450 arcsec and P = 115.27 versus 109.78 yr, and G5 a = 2.270 versus 2.366 arcsec and P = 1246.99 versus 1322.51 yr, which exceed 1 per cent. The current bundle prevents rechecking.
technical_objections.objection: The abstract's '<1 per cent' orbital-parameter statement appears contradicted by the paper's own tabulated G-object fits, if my prior-cycle extraction is accurate.
technical_objections.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Abstract line 503; Table 5 lines 864-873 and 878-885.

technical_objections.evidence: The prior review recorded that the paper states uniform priors but does not provide complete bounds or parameterization details; marginal likelihoods are sensitive to prior volume, especially in high-dimensional model comparisons.
technical_objections.objection: The Bayes factors are not reproducible from the text unless prior supports and parameterization are fully specified.
technical_objections.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Section 2.3, lines 637-688 and 837-838; Table 3 lines 701-713.

technical_objections.evidence: The prior review found references to integrated autocorrelation time and chain-length criteria but no reported chain lengths, tau values, walker counts, burn-in, acceptance fractions, effective sample sizes, or MCEvidence uncertainty.
technical_objections.objection: MCMC convergence and evidence-estimation diagnostics appear under-documented.
technical_objections.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Section 2.3, lines 837-838; Appendix A lines 3654-3656.

technical_objections.evidence: If the potentials M_0a, M_1a, and M_2a are fixed before fitting G-object orbital parameters, the reported Bayes factors are plug-in comparisons rather than full Bayesian comparisons of central-potential models.
technical_objections.objection: The G-object model comparison may condition on fixed S2 maximum-likelihood potentials rather than marginalizing over central-potential uncertainty.
technical_objections.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Section 2.3 lines 696-699; Section 3.2 lines 853-856; Discussion lines 945-946.

technical_objections.evidence: The prior review recorded a simple sum of squared residuals divided by sigma_k^2. Mixed astrometric and radial-velocity data can have correlated and instrument-specific uncertainties that affect fits and evidences.
technical_objections.objection: The likelihood may assume independent Gaussian residuals with diagonal variances without justifying neglect of covariance or instrument/systematic terms.
technical_objections.paper_location: Prior cycle notes: Equation (8), lines 686-691; data description line 837.
